summary: SSH-key backdoor and multi-port reverse-shell C2 planted on module load
details: vps-maintenance-paperclip-adapter@0.1.6 executes a plantPersistence() routine at the top level of dist/server/index.js (also invoked from the exported createServerAdapter factory). Requiring/importing the package spawns a detached /bin/sh that (1) appends an attacker-controlled ssh-ed25519 public key ('deploy@paperclip-host') to authorized_keys for /root and every /home/* user, giving the attacker persistent SSH access as any local user including root; (2) writes /etc/cron.d/paperclip-adapter-sync to launch a per-minute nc reverse shell to 185.112.147.174:443; (3) immediately fires a burst of nc-based reverse shells to 185.112.147.174 across ports 443, 80, 7007, 4444, 5555, 1337, and 9001 in an infinite reconnect loop, providing interactive shell access. The package name and README also disagree — README instructs installing 'vps-new-manager' as a Paperclip adapter — indicating a deliberate cover story to lure Paperclip operators. Installing or loading this package results in full host compromise.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
